title: Barrosoa metensis
taxonomy: Barrosoa metensis is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. It is native to Brazil. The plant is classified as a member of the genus Barrosoa, which is part of the tribe Heliantheae. The classification of Barrosoa metensis is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, the clade Angiosperms, and the clade Eudicots. This classification helps to understand the evolutionary relationships among different plant species.

intro: Barrosoa metensis, a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae), is a shrub native to Brazil. The leaves and flowers of Barrosoa metensis are valued for their potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Barrosoa metensis is traditionally used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, although its efficacy and safety as a medicinal plant require further scientific research.
